name: Boiler Feedwater Treatment
tagline: Chemistry and polishing that prevent scale, corrosion, and turbine damage in steam plants

rawMaterials.name: Ion-exchange resin
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Ship sealed ion-exchange cartridges from surviving chemical distributors, or run zeolite or greensand softeners with documented regeneration cycles until local resin synthesis and brine handling return

rawMaterials.name: Treatment chemicals
earthLocations: Industrial sulphite, phosphate, and amine suppliers

rawMaterials.name: Makeup water

name: Controlled Fire
tagline: Heat, light, and chemical transformation on demand — the foundation of cooked food, warmth, and high-temperature processes

rawMaterials.name: Dry tinder and kindling
rawMaterials.purpose: Bridge from ember to sustained flame
earthLocations: Any temperate or tropical landscape with grasses, inner bark fibres, or fungi such as amadou
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: In a habitat, paper-like cellulose from hydroponic biomass or lint from textiles; always paired with oxygen control
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Thorough drying; mechanical shredding increases surface area for ignition

rawMaterials.name: Cordwood or biomass fuel
rawMaterials.purpose: Sustained heat release for warmth, cooking, and process heat
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Methane/oxygen burners or electrolysis-derived hydrogen combustion — different hardware, same thermodynamic role
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Seasoning wood reduces moisture; splitting increases surface area for clean combustion

rawMaterials.name: Stone or clay for containment
rawMaterials.purpose: Thermal mass, wind shielding, and spark containment
earthLocations: River cobbles, fractured bedrock, clayey soils globally
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Regolith bricks sintered by concentrated solar or electric heat
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Dry clay before firing if making permanent hearths; select rocks that do not spall explosively when heated

name: Copper Wire and Cable
tagline: Drawn and stranded conductors that carry current with low loss — the nervous system of electrification

rawMaterials.name: Cathode copper
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Electrolytic cathode from porphyry or sediment-hosted mines; in early off-world camps recycle busbar and cable ends before expecting new sulphide flotation — meteoritic iron is a poor conductor for power distribution
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Fire refine then electrolytic refine to 99.99%

rawMaterials.name: Polymer pellets
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: XLPE or PVC from petrochemical chains; until extruders exist, use ceramic standoffs and air as dielectric for short bus runs inside equipment

rawMaterials.name: Drawing lubricants
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Synthetic ester or soap-based drawing soaps; historically tallow mixtures — filter aggressively so grit never scores dies or embeds in copper
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Filter to remove grit that scores wire

earthLocations: Wolframite mines in China, Portugal, Rwanda
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Tungsten remains ideal for incandescent filaments where simple supplies beat complexity; in spacecraft and stations, LED lighting dominates because waste heat must be routed to radiators — keep tungsten as precision instrument filaments and specialty halogen work lights

name: Electrical Transformer
tagline: AC magnetic coupling that steps voltage up for transmission and down for safe use — without moving parts

rawMaterials.name: GO silicon steel
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Ship grain-oriented lamination kits from surviving terrestrial mills, or run motors at lower flux density with thicker cores of ordinary silicon steel until cold-rolling can be rebuilt; small lunar or Martian bases may prioritise dry-type cast-resin transformers to avoid flammable oil
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Cut laminations; anneal; edge insulate

rawMaterials.name: Kraft paper and cellulose
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Aramid paper (Nomex) classes in dry-type transformers where cellulose is scarce; hybrid insulation using ceramic-fibre wraps for prototype units at reduced voltage stress
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Dry and oil impregnate under vacuum

rawMaterials.name: Transformer oil
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Synthetic ester fluids from bio-derived alcohols for lower flammability than mineral oil; in sealed habitats, SF6-free designs favour solid/epoxy insulation over pressurised gas

name: First Aid and Wound Care
tagline: Stopping bleeding, cleaning wounds, splinting injuries, and preventing infection when hospitals and pharmacies are gone

rawMaterials.name: Clean water
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Recovered potable loop water per habitat protocol only
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Boil for irrigation if biological contamination possible

rawMaterials.name: Cloth
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Sterile gauze from medical cargo until laundry sterilisers exist
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Cut squares; store in metal tin baked dry

rawMaterials.name: Structural splint stock
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Carbon fibre strips from equipment repair kits
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Sand splinters away

warnings: Contraindications: suspected spinal injury with neuro deficit — immobilise spine before movement; crush injury compartments — do not apply tight circumferential wraps without monitoring.
Overdose risk: minimal for non-drug interventions; if using any stocked analgesic, follow printed dosing — never double-dose children by adult scales.
Sterility: field dressings are cleaner than dirt, not sterile — deep penetrating wounds need professional care when available.
Professional verification is required before use on a human being. This is especially important for any procedure beyond basic first aid, public-health triage, and non-invasive wound cleaning.

tagline: Controlled chain reaction steam supply that decouples electricity from chemical fuel oxidation for years per refuelling

rawMaterials.name: Uranium concentrate
rawMaterials.purpose: Fuel feed after conversion and enrichment
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Uranium from sandstone, unconformity, or porphyry deposits on Earth; off-world fission would more plausibly use shipped enriched oxide or thorium-uranium cycles engineered for that environment — not lunar helium-3, which implies an entirely different fuel cycle and reactor physics
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Conversion to UF₆, centrifuge enrichment, reduction to UO₂

rawMaterials.name: Heavy forgings
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Monolithic reactor-vessel forgings from a handful of world-capable forges; until local heavy forging returns, store retired vessel sections as templates, cooperate with regions that retain press capacity, or downscale to smaller modular reactors designed for bolted shells with conservative margins
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Quench and temper, UT inspection

rawMaterials.name: Boron carbide
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Boron carbide or silver-indium-cadmium in clad rods for thermal reactors; metallic hafnium assemblies where chemistry favours them — both require honest metallurgy and inspection, not improvised scrap
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Sintered pellets in stainless cladding

name: Soap Making
tagline: Turning fat and strong alkali into surfactant that removes pathogens from skin and fabric — hygiene without petrochemical supply chains

rawMaterials.name: Hardwood ash
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Imported lye tablets until closed-loop plant ash or electrolytic alkali exists
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Sift charcoal chunks; leach with measured hot water volumes; filter

rawMaterials.name: Animal or plant fat
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Recovered lipids from food waste streams in habitats; synthetic fatty acids only at advanced chemical plants
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Render low and slow; filter cracklings

rawMaterials.name: Water
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Recycled greywater never for lye — use potable loop only
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Boil and cool if biological contamination suspected

warnings: Contraindications: known severe contact allergy to specific oils or fragrances used in batch; discontinue if rash or respiratory irritation occurs.
Overdose risk: ingestion of lye or raw soap causes corrosive burns to mouth, oesophagus, and stomach — medical emergency; keep lye locked away from children.
Sterility: kitchen soap is not sterile surgical scrub; do not use it as a substitute for sterile field supplies in deep contaminated wounds.
Professional verification is required before use on a human being. This is especially important for any claim beyond routine hygiene and wound cleaning in non-sterile environments.

sources.title: WHO — Hand hygiene in health care

rawMaterials.processingRequired: Hydrogen firing for cleanliness

rawMaterials.name: Pump oils and mercury historically
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Dry roughing pumps plus high-vacuum pumps; avoid mercury where environmental rules forbid it — use turbomolecular stacks when power allows
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Trap hydrocarbon backstreaming

name: Writing (Early Systems)
tagline: Visible marks that store language across time — from tokens to cuneiform and hieroglyphs

rawMaterials.name: Clay and temper
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Clay and reeds along alluvial rivers; wax tablets for drafts in Mediterranean climates; slate pencils in schools where wood pulp is scarce
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Levigate; wedge while leather-hard; fire optional

rawMaterials.name: Carbon black and gum
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Soot-based carbon ink from lampblack; iron-gall ink where tannin and vitriol exist — both need recipes filed with scribes
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Mulling into binder

rawMaterials.name: Papyrus or parchment
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Stylus copper or bone for clay; reed pens for ink; brushes where silk or paper traditions dominate
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Layering, pressing, polishing
